authority_answers.who_may_authorize_implementation_creatio: Human operator only, through explicit replay-visible approval artifacts.
authority_answers.how_generated_files_are_represented: IMPLEMENTATION_MANIFEST_ARTIFACT_V1 file_entries with exact path, artifact type, operation, content encoding, content hash, preflight state, validation requirements, and false authority flags.
authority_answers.how_content_hashes_are_bound: Per-file content hashes are bound across manifest, validation, content acceptance, filesystem mutation authorization, post-application verification, and certification.
authority_answers.how_tests_are_bound: test_entries link generated tests to implementation file entries, validation commands, fixture references, expected coverage target, and negative case requirements.
authority_answers.how_implementation_manifests_are_represe: A non-authoritative content-bearing IMPLEMENTATION_MANIFEST_ARTIFACT_V1 with ordered file_entries, ordered test_entries, validation requirements, forbidden operations, manifest_hash, and artifact_hash.
authority_answers.what_replay_evidence_is_required: Candidate selection, provider generation authorization, provider request/response, manifest, code validation, test validation, content acceptance, filesystem mutation authorization, file application, post-application verification, and implementation certification.
authority_answers.how_multi_file_implementations_are_certi: By IMPLEMENTATION_MANIFEST_CERTIFICATION_ARTIFACT_V1 verifying manifest hash continuity, per-file hashes, per-test hashes, validation results, human acceptance, mutation authorization, file application results, post-application hashes, and replay reconstruction.
